mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 7.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings revisions downward, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicate a supply-demand mismatch where inventory drawdowns outpace consumption, compressing corporate margins and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when INDPRO falls > 1.5% month-over-month while RSAFS rises > 5% month-over-month, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, elevated core inflation readings force the market to price in prolonged higher-for-longer rates, which compresses equity multiples and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity multiples expand despite CPIAUCSL rising > 0.4% month-over-month alongside FEDFUNDS hikes, or the forward return differential between high-inflation/tightening regimes and baseline is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically supporting positive equity forward returns over a 5-trading-day window as corporate profit growth translates directly into price appreciation.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth decelerates while SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 70% of observations, or the rolling Spearman correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward returns falls below 0.10 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A breakout in industrial production above 100 index points signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization and healthy order backlogs, which lifts related sector earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns turn negative following three consecutive months of industrial production above 100.0, or the signal's hit rate drops below 45% in a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strong payroll growth and rising real GDP indicate a self-reinforcing economic expansion that sustains consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns when PAYEMS adds > 200k jobs and GDPC1 grows > 2.5% annualized, or the interaction coefficient in a multivariate forward return model becomes negative and statistically significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a credit-constrained environment, a sharp deceleration in consumer credit balances reduces household purchasing power, forcing analysts to downgrade near-term earnings estimates and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive despite a > 3% monthly contraction in consumer credit balances, or the marginal effect of RSAFS declines on forward returns becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero across three rolling 12-month windows.
